info.title: Alchemy API
info.description:
API contract for Alchemy — an AI-driven recipe creation and management platform.
Covers the user-facing v1 API at api.cookwithalchemy.com and the admin API at admin.cookwithalchemy.com.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Response projection for ingredient and inline measurement units.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Optional ingredient grouping strategy for response rendering.

parameters.name: inline_measurements
parameters.description: Whether to append inline measurements directly inside step instructions.

sourceFlat.summary: Source units with flat ingredient list

value.title: Weeknight Pasta
value.description: A weeknight pasta that lands with the confidence of a corner trattoria classic, all bright tomatoes, olive oil, and basil in a sauce that tastes far more patient than the clock would suggest.

metricComponentGrouped.summary: Metric units with component grouping and inline measurements

value.title: Weeknight Pasta
value.description: A weeknight pasta that lands with the confidence of a corner trattoria classic, all bright tomatoes, olive oil, and basil in a sauce that tastes far more patient than the clock would suggest.

get.summary: Get recipe version history with causal chat/thread context
User cookbook with canonical + variant data
get.description:
Returns the user's cookbook entries. Each entry includes canonical recipe
preview data plus variant status. When a variant exists, the preview
reflects the personalised version (title stays canonical, summary and
tags come from the variant).

post.summary: Search public recipes for Explore and shared retrieval
post.description:
Canonical discovery endpoint for recipe search and Explore feed pagination.
When both `query` and `preset_id` are omitted or blank, this returns the Explore feed using the same cursor-backed search session infrastructure.

post.summary: Save a canonical recipe to cookbook and optionally trigger variant materialisation
post.description:
Creates a cookbook_entries row for the canonical recipe. If autopersonalize
is true (default) and the user has relevant constraint preferences, queues
async variant materialisation. Returns the cookbook entry state.

post.summary: Ingest first-party product behavior telemetry
post.description:
Batched client-side telemetry sink for Explore, Chat, Cookbook, and
recipe detail behavior events. Events are append-only and validated
against the canonical event catalog.

delete.description: Deletes the cookbook entry and associated variant data for this recipe.

post.summary: Create and attach a child recipe (side/appetizer/etc)

patch.summary: Update attachment position or relation type

get.summary: Get the user's personalised variant for a canonical recipe
get.description:
Returns the user's private variant of a canonical recipe, including the
personalised payload, adaptation summary, tag diff, and provenance data.
Returns 404 if the user has no variant for this recipe.

404.description: No variant exists for this user and recipe

post.summary: Create or refresh the user's variant for a canonical recipe
post.description:
Materialises (or re-materialises) the user's private variant from the
canonical recipe base plus the user's current preference profile. If the
variant has manual edits, attempts to reapply them after re-personalisation.
If reapplication conflicts, returns variant with status needs_review.

post.summary: Publish a private variant as a new canonical recipe
post.description:
Creates a new canonical recipe lineage from the user's private variant.
The new canonical has a derived_from graph edge to the original. The
variant remains in the user's cookbook unchanged.

title.description: Optional new title for the published recipe. If omitted, uses the original canonical title.

post.summary: Create collection
Add recipe to collection

get.summary: Get onboarding completion state for authenticated user

post.summary: Continue onboarding interview and persist preference updates

get.summary: Get active user memories and snapshot

post.summary: Soft-delete a specific memory
Reset all user memories
Process pending image jobs for authenticated user
Run an ephemeral two-lane image simulation compare

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description:
When set to `1`, returns `application/x-ndjson` stream events as each lane completes,
followed by a final `result` event. Omit for the normal JSON response.

post.summary: Process due recipe metadata jobs (internal authenticated scope)
Re-queue a metadata job safely (internal authenticated scope)
Recompute metadata for a recipe or recipe version (internal authenticated scope)
Recompute metadata for a targeted scope (internal authenticated scope)

leaked_only.description: Limit scope to leaked rows (`needs_retry` + `ingredient_id IS NOT NULL`).

current_versions_only.description: Restrict scope to current recipe versions.

post.summary: Process due memory extraction jobs (internal authenticated scope)
Re-queue a memory job safely (internal authenticated scope)
Import a recipe from a URL, pasted text, or cookbook photo
post.description:
Accepts a recipe source and returns a seeded ChatSession with a
CandidateRecipeSet, ready for the standard Generate flow (iterate
via POST /chat/{id}/messages, commit via POST /chat/{id}/commit).

Images are always re-generated — source images are never stored.
The first draft is a source-faithful rewrite in Alchemy wording.

Supports fingerprint-based dedup: re-importing the same URL or
text returns the existing chat session if one was already created.

value.text:
Classic Margherita Pizza

Ingredients:
- 1 pizza dough
- 1/2 cup tomato sauce
- 8 oz fresh mozzarella
- Fresh basil leaves
- 2 tbsp olive oil

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 500°F
2. Stretch dough on floured surface
3. Spread sauce, add torn mozzarella
4. Bake 10-12 minutes until crust is golden
5. Top with fresh basil and drizzle olive oil

photo.summary: Import from a cookbook page photo

200.description: Import successful — chat session seeded with recipe candidate

post.summary: Start a new chat session

assistant_reply.text: Do you want this to be creamy or tomato-forward?

outOfScope.summary: Out-of-scope ask is refused and redirected to cooking

assistant_reply.text: I can’t help with that here. I can help with recipes or cooking techniques.

post.summary: Add a message to chat session thread

assistant_reply.text: Here are two options to start from.

recipe.title: Spicy Chicken Parm
recipe.summary: Spicy chicken parm with crisp edges.
recipe.description: This chicken parm arrives with the swagger of a red-sauce darling and just enough chile to keep the whole affair from getting too comfortable. It is crunchy, saucy, and unapologetically dramatic in the best possible way.

recipe.title: Garlicky Broccolini
recipe.description: The broccolini comes in all snap and sparkle, charred at the edges and lifted with enough garlic and lemon to feel like more than a side dish afterthought.

assistant_reply.text: Updated with a lighter breading and shorter timing.

recipe.title: Lighter Spicy Chicken Parm
recipe.description: The lighter version keeps the sauce-slick charm of chicken parm but trades heft for a sharper, brisker kind of pleasure, the sort of plate that feels lively rather than leaden.

get.summary: Fetch graph entities and edges related to a recipe
Fetch graph entities and edges related to an ingredient
Get trending ingredients by popularity or substitution momentum

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sort mode. "trending" ranks by recipe-derived popularity,
"momentum" ranks by substitution velocity (ingredients being swapped in/out).

post.summary: Refresh recipe popularity scores and ingredient trending stats
post.description:
Admin batch job that recomputes all recipe popularity scores
(save_count, variant_count, view_count, popularity_score, trending_score)
and ingredient trending stats (recipe-derived popularity + substitution momentum).

SimModelOverrides.name: x-sim-model-overrides
SimModelOverrides.description:
Optional JSON string used by Admin Simulation Runner to override model/provider per scope.
Format:
{
  "chat_ideation":{"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-3-5-haiku-latest"},
  "ingredient_enrich":{"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5-mini"}
}
Common scope keys:
chat_ideation, chat_generation, chat_iteration, classify,
ingredient_alias_normalize, ingredient_phrase_split, ingredient_enrich,
recipe_metadata_enrich, ingredient_relation_infer, preference_normalize, equipment_filter,
onboarding, image, image_quality_eval, memory_extract, memory_select, memory_summarize, memory_conflict_resolve.
Not required for normal mobile clients.

ImportRequest.description:
Discriminated union for recipe import sources. Exactly one of
url/text/photo_asset_ref must be provided, matching the kind field.

photo_asset_ref.description: Storage reference for the uploaded cookbook page photo

LlmScope.description: Internal LLM pipeline scope key used for prompt/rule/model routing.

summary.description: Short preview/share blurb used in cards, search, and share text.

description.description: Long-form editorial detail copy shown on recipe detail surfaces.
Long-form editorial detail copy shown on recipe detail surfaces.

summary.description: Short preview/share blurb used in cards, search, and share text.

save_count.description: Number of users who saved this recipe. Only present in search/explore responses when > 0.

variant_count.description: Number of personalized variants created. Only present in search/explore responses when > 0.

popularity_score.description: All-time weighted discovery score used to identify broadly popular recipes.

trending_score.description: Recent weighted discovery score used to identify recipes gaining momentum.

sort_by.description: Sort order for the Explore feed. Only applies when query and preset_id are empty.

preference_updates.description: Preference changes extracted from this chat turn. iOS synthesises inline system cards from this.

variant_id.description: The creator's private variant ID, if variant was materialised.

variant_version_id.description: The creator's private variant version ID, if variant was materialised.

VariantStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of a user's recipe variant.
current = up to date, stale = needs re-personalisation,
processing = re-personalisation in progress, failed = retryable failure,
needs_review = manual edits conflict with new constraints,
none = no variant exists (user sees canonical).

VariantTags.description:
Structured tags computed from the variant's personalized content.
Used for multi-dimensional cookbook filtering. Empty object if no
variant exists. Tags are re-computed on every variant materialization.

technique.description: Cooking technique tags (e.g. grilling, braising, sous-vide).

time_minutes.description: Total cook time in minutes. Null if unknown.

difficulty.description: Difficulty level (easy, medium, complex). Null if unknown.

key_ingredients.description: Top ingredients from the variant (lowercased, max 8).

summary.description: Variant summary if variant exists, otherwise canonical summary.

personalized_at.description: When the variant was last materialised. Null if no variant.

autopersonalize.description: Whether to auto-personalise the recipe using the user's constraint preferences.

source_surface.description: Optional originating UI surface for first-party attribution (for example explore or cookbook).

event_id.description: Client-generated idempotency key for the behavior event.

surface.description: Optional explicit surface override. When omitted, the API applies the canonical surface for the event type.

adaptation_summary.description: Natural language summary of what was adapted and why.

provenance.description: Structured record of applied constraints, preferences, and manual edits.

trending_recipe_count.description: Recipes containing this ingredient with trending_score > 0.

popularity_score.description: Sum of popularity_score from recipes containing this ingredient.

trending_score.description: Sum of trending_score from recipes containing this ingredient.

sub_in_count.description: All-time count of this ingredient being swapped IN as a replacement.

sub_out_count.description: All-time count of this ingredient being swapped OUT (replaced by another).

momentum_score.description:
7-day substitution momentum. Scaled -100 to +100.
Positive = rising ingredient (being used as replacement more often).
Negative = declining ingredient (being replaced more often).

SubstitutionDiff.description:
A single ingredient substitution made during personalization.
Records what was swapped, the replacement, the driving constraint,
and a human-readable reason.

original.description: The canonical ingredient that was replaced.

replacement.description: The ingredient used in the variant.

constraint.description: The user constraint that triggered the substitution (e.g. "gluten-free").

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the substitution was made.

substitution_diffs.description: Structured ingredient substitutions made during personalization.

conflicts.description: Manual edits that conflict with current constraints.

field.description: Which preference field changed (e.g. equipment, dietary_restrictions).

value.description: The specific value that was added, removed, or updated.

category.description: How this preference is classified for propagation.
